title: Hierarchy of UX Goals
description: A visual breakdown of how high-level user and business goals translate into actionable design objectives and measurable outcomes.
elements: Business Goals (e.g., Conversion, Retention, Brand Loyalty)
UX Objectives (e.g., Reduce Steps, Improve Feedback, Simplify Navigation)
Measurable Outcomes (e.g., Task Success Rate, Time on Task, Error Rate, Satisfaction Score)
relationships: User and Business Goals inform and align to create UX Objectives, which are then measured by specific Outcomes to validate success
